shortdesc.ph: Plays the specified local or online sound effect file.
Plays a specified local or online sound effect file.

p.codeblock: virtual int playEffect(int soundId, const char* filePath, int loopCount, double pitch, double pan, int gain, bool publish = false, int startPos = 0) = 0;
public abstract int playEffect(int soundId, String filePath, int loopCount, double pitch, double pan, double gain, boolean publish, int startPos);

section.p: To play multiple sound effect files simultaneously, call this method multiple times with different soundId and filePath. For the best user experience, Agora recommends not playing more than three sound effect files at the same time.

This method supports playing URI files starting with content://. For supported audio file formats, see What formats of audio files does the Agora RTC SDK support. If the local audio file does not exist, the SDK does not support the file format, or the URL of the audio file cannot be accessed, the SDK returns AUDIO_MIXING_REASON_CAN_NOT_OPEN.
section.note: If you need to play online sound effect files, Agora recommends caching them to the local device first, preloading them into memory with , and then calling this method to play the sound effect. Otherwise, playback may fail or be silent due to loading timeout or failure. If is called before , the file resource will not be closed after is executed. The next call to will start playback from the beginning. If is not called before , the resource will be destroyed after is executed. The next call to will attempt to reopen the file and play from the beginning.

ul.li: If you need to play an online sound effect file, it is recommended to cache it to the local device first, call preloadEffect to preload the file into memory, and then call this method to play it. Otherwise, playback may fail or be silent due to timeout or failure in loading.
If you call preloadEffectEx before calling playEffectEx, the file resource will not be closed after playback. The next time you call playEffectEx, it will start playing from the beginning.

If you call playEffectEx without first calling preloadEffectEx, the resource will be destroyed after playback. The next time you call playEffectEx, it will attempt to reopen the file and play from the beginning.

section.title: Timing
section.p: You can call this method before or after joining a channel.
You can call this method before or after joining a channel.

section.title: Related Callbacks
section.p: After this method is successfully called, the SDK triggers the callback to report the completion of sound effect playback.
After the sound effect file is played, the SDK triggers the callback.

plentry.pd: Sound effect ID. Each sound effect file has a unique ID. If is called to preload the sound effect into memory, ensure this parameter matches the soundId in .

File path. Supports URL and absolute path of local files. The path must include the file name and extension. Supported audio formats include MP3, AAC, M4A, MP4, WAV, and 3GP. See [What audio file formats does Agora RTC SDK support](https://doc.shengwang.cn/faq/general-product-inquiry/audio-format). If is called to preload the sound effect into memory, ensure this parameter matches the filePath in .

Number of times to loop the sound effect:
≥ 0: Number of times to play. For example, 1 means loop once, i.e., play twice in total.
-1: Loop the sound effect indefinitely.
Pitch of the sound effect. Range: 0.5 to 2.0. The default value is 1.0, which represents the original pitch. The smaller the value, the lower the pitch.

Spatial position of the sound effect. Range: -1.0 to 1.0:
-1.0: Sound effect comes from the user's left.
0.0: Sound effect comes from the front.
1.0: Sound effect comes from the user's right.
Volume of the sound effect. Range: 0.0 to 100.0. The default value is 100.0, which represents the original volume. The smaller the value, the lower the volume.

Whether to publish the sound effect to remote users:
: Publish the sound effect to remote users. Both local and remote users can hear it.
: (Default) Do not publish the sound effect to remote users. Only the local user can hear it.
Start position of the sound effect file in milliseconds.

Sound effect ID. Each sound effect file must have a unique ID. If you have preloaded the sound effect into memory by calling preloadEffect, make sure this parameter value is the same as soundId in preloadEffect.

Path to the sound effect file. Supports URIs starting with content://, paths starting with /assets/, URLs, and absolute paths to local files. The path must be accurate to the file name and extension. Supported audio formats include MP3, AAC, M4A, MP4, WAV, and 3GP. If you have preloaded the sound effect into memory by calling preloadEffect, make sure this parameter value is the same as filePath in preloadEffect.

Number of times the sound effect loops:
≥ 0: Number of times to play. For example, 1 means loop once, i.e., play twice in total.
-1: Play the audio file in an infinite loop.
Pitch of the sound effect. The range is 0.5 to 2.0. The default value is 1.0, which means the original pitch. The smaller the value, the lower the pitch.

Spatial position of the sound effect. The range is -1.0 to 1.0:
-1.0: The sound effect plays from the user's left.
0.0: The sound effect plays from the user's front.
1.0: The sound effect plays from the user's right.
Volume of the sound effect. The range is 0.0 to 100.0. The default value is 100.0, which means the original volume. The smaller the value, the lower the volume.

Whether to publish the sound effect to remote users:
: Publish the sound effect to remote users, both local and remote users can hear it.
: Do not publish the sound effect to remote users, only local users can hear it.
Starting position of the sound effect file playback, in milliseconds.
